task.title: Create a Project
task.shortdesc: Add a new project to your portfolio and configure its metadata.

context.p: Create a project for each distinct commitment in your portfolio. A commitment qualifies as a project if it has a meaningful end state, benefits from regular sessions, and would suffer from being deprioritized indefinitely.

step.cmd: In the Project field, enter a short, recognizable name.

info.p: Use a name you will recognize at a glance on the Dashboard. For example: "Novel Draft," "Home Studio Album," or "AWS Certification."

step.cmd: In the Description field, enter a one- or two-sentence summary of the project's purpose and desired outcome.

choices.choice: Select Active if you intend to schedule sessions for this project this week.
Select Backlog if you are capturing the project but are not ready to start yet.

step.cmd: Set the Priority field to a value from 1 (highest) to 5 (lowest).

info.p: Priority is for your reference. It does not affect scoring or display order.

step.cmd: (Optional) Enter a Start date and an End date in YYYY-MM-DD format, or click Today to fill in today's date.

step.stepresult: The project appears in the Projects list. If status is Active, it also appears on the Dashboard.

postreq.p: After creating a project, add milestones to define its key outcomes. See .
